mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and increases debt-service costs, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rising FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that reduces household income and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in CPI inflation and the federal funds rate creates a joint monetary tightening shock that aggressively reprices equity risk premiums upward, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform the linear sum of individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in real GDP growth signals a demand recovery that supports corporate revenue expansion and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth surprises fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in post-shock recovery periods with a hit rate below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production acceleration indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO is followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction and downstream demand for materials and credit, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumer spending power, which supports aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS surprises are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer and investment demand resilience, which reduces recession probability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and HOUST yields a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive co-movement fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances.
